title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: US special forces conducted a confirmed combat search-and-rescue operation inside Iran on April 4, extracting both crewmembers from a downed F-15E jet—the first verified US ground entry in the ongoing 2026 Iran war, which began with joint US-Israel airstrikes on February 28 targeting nuclear sites and military infrastructure. President Trump has issued ultimatums for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, threatening strikes on power plants and potential seizure of Kharg Island, prompting Marine and Airborne reinforcements to the region amid preparations for limited ground actions. Iran vows retaliation against any boots-on-the-ground escalation, while air and missile exchanges continue. Traders assess rescue missions as qualifying entries versus full invasion thresholds, with deadlines and diplomatic stalls heightening uncertainty.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's ultimatum demands Iran accept a ceasefire by an imminent deadline—threatening devastating strikes on bridges, power plants, and oil infrastructure—or face escalation, following recent U.S. airstrikes on military targets at Iran's Kharg Island hub. Tehran has rejected temporary proposals, including Pakistan-mediated 45-day and two-week plans plus a U.S. 15-point outline, insisting on a permanent war end and denying direct talks in favor of indirect channels. Amid vows of intensified Iranian attacks and stalled diplomacy, trader consensus reflects dim prospects absent a last-minute breakthrough, with risks of broader conflict over the Strait of Hormuz and regional proxies looming large.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 16% implied probability for the 2026 FIFA World Cup title, driven by their unbeaten UEFA qualifying run capped by a dramatic penalty shootout win over Netherlands in late March play-offs, plus solid friendlies like 3-0 over Serbia, aligning with their No. 2 FIFA ranking. France trails closely at 13.5% after their own play-off triumph versus Croatia on pens and a 2-1 friendly victory against Brazil, reclaiming the top FIFA spot. England (11.5%), Argentina (9.2% as CONMEBOL leaders), and Brazil (8.6%) cluster tightly amid mixed pre-tournament form—England drawing Uruguay but losing to Japan—highlighting parity in the expanded 48-team field, recent group draw balance, and uncertainties in depth, travel across North America, and peaking fitness ahead of the June kickoff.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 7, 2026 between Real Madrid CF and FC Bayern München.

markets.question: Will Real Madrid CF win on 2026-04-07?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 7, 2026
If Real Madrid CF wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Bayern München's 2-1 victory over Real Madrid CF in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg at the Bernabéu has driven trader consensus to price Bayern's win at 100% implied probability, aligning with the official result confirmed post-match. Bayern capitalized on early momentum with Luis Díaz's opener in the 41st minute and Harry Kane's quick second-half strike at the 46th, showcasing clinical finishing and defensive resolve despite Kylian Mbappé's 74th-minute consolation goal for Madrid. This away win gives Bayern a vital aggregate lead ahead of the April 15 return leg in Munich, where Real Madrid would need a multi-goal turnaround. Rare challenges like official score disputes or procedural reviews could theoretically shift resolution, but none are indicated.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Governor Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 25.7% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination amid a fragmented field reflecting post-2024 party regrouping, bolstered by his executive record, national fundraising strength, and visibility from high-profile clashes with Republican governors. Recent Echelon and polling averages favor Kamala Harris higher, but markets discount her at 4.3% due to her 2024 loss, emphasizing electability concerns. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's 8.7% stems from progressive base energy and youth appeal, while Jon Ossoff's 5.7% highlights his swing-state Georgia profile. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm results in battlegrounds, key endorsements, and fundraising hauls before primaries ramp up.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler's status as world No. 1 and two-time Masters champion (2022, 2024) with elite Augusta course history—strong ball-striking and par-5 scoring—positions him as trader consensus favorite amid a wide-open field, though his odds reflect waning betting support after a less dominant early 2026 and a shaky Players Championship cut make. Defending champion Rory McIlroy eyes back-to-back green jackets, a feat last achieved by Tiger Woods in 2001-02, but recent back injury withdrawals from the Arnold Palmer Invitational raise fitness doubts despite solid practice rounds. Jon Rahm's flawless top-five finishes including a Hong Kong win, paired with Bryson DeChambeau's back-to-back LIV triumphs, have shortened their prices, fueling the tight top-five bunching as no player enters with peak momentum, deep contenders like Xander Schauffele and Ludvig Aberg lurk, and withdrawals of Phil Mickelson and Tiger Woods thin the legends without shifting the balance.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a narrow edge at 17.6% in Polymarket's 2028 presidential winner market, reflecting trader consensus on his incumbency advantage and MAGA continuity as Trump's heir apparent, while California Governor Gavin Newsom trails closely at 16.6% amid Democrats' search for a post-2024 standard-bearer. Recent weeks saw Vance's odds soften from earlier highs due to ambiguous statements on his intentions and emerging GOP rivalry from Secretary of State Marco Rubio (10.2%), whose foreign policy profile surged in March amid diplomatic wins like Venezuela developments. The race remains tightly contested with no dominant frontrunner, as 2026 midterms loom to test party control, swing state strength, fundraising, and early primary polling—any Trump endorsement, scandal, or national crisis could widen gaps.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $200 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (LOW) $20 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil spot prices traded around $110–115 per barrel on April 7, 2026, reflecting a sharp rally fueled by escalating Middle East geopolitical risks, including Strait of Hormuz tensions and U.S. threats against Iran under President Trump, which have embedded a hefty risk premium despite bearish U.S. crude inventory builds exceeding expectations in the EIA's latest Weekly Petroleum Status Report for the week ending March 27. OPEC+ members, including Saudi Arabia and Russia, opted for a modest 206,000 bpd production hike starting this month, yet resilient global demand from economic recovery has sustained upward momentum with year-to-date gains over 80%. Traders monitor weekly EIA releases every Wednesday, potential supply disruptions, and futures curve flattening through April settlement.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. leads trader consensus at 49% implied probability for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination on Polymarket, buoyed by his recent midterm travel push promoting the "Make America Healthy Again" agenda as a top Trump surrogate and family endorsements signaling his intent to run, despite low favorability polls. Vice President J.D. Vance holds 38% amid reports of his indecision on a presidential bid during foreign policy trips like his Hungary visit, with odds slipping from prior highs. Secretary of State Marco Rubio's 22% reflects gains from his Iran war handling and GOP donor preferences over Vance in informal Trump polling, positioning the race as closely contested ahead of 2026 midterms that could reshape primary dynamics.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: US and Israeli strikes on Iranian oil facilities, rail lines, bridges, and power plants intensified over the past week, prompting Tehran to reject a US-proposed 45-day ceasefire and mediators' last-ditch efforts amid President Trump's looming deadline and threats of further devastation. The conflict, sparked by nearly 900 US-Israeli airstrikes on February 28 targeting Iran's nuclear sites, missile program, and military infrastructure, has seen Iran retaliate with missile barrages on Israel, close the Strait of Hormuz, and dismiss a prior 15-point US peace plan as unreasonable. Ongoing UN Security Council discussions and regional diplomacy offer slim hopes for de-escalation, but persistent military actions signal trader consensus on prolonged hostilities absent major breakthroughs.

title: Sporting CP vs. Arsenal FC
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 7, 2026 between Sporting CP and Arsenal FC.

markets.question: Will Sporting CP win on 2026-04-07?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 7, 2026
If Sporting CP wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's commanding 100% implied probability stems from their gritty 1-0 away victory over Sporting CP in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg, clinched by Kai Havertz's stoppage-time strike at Estádio José Alvalade. Sporting's early dominance in shots failed to breach Arsenal's stout defense anchored by Saliba and Gabriel, while pre-match absences of captain Morten Hjulmand (suspended) and winger Nuno Santos (injured) eroded the hosts' midfield control and width. Arsenal's recent 5-1 group-stage thrashing of Sporting and superior Premier League form further solidified trader consensus. Realistic challenges include a belated VAR review overturning the goal or administrative dispute, though official reports confirm the result.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' George Russell and rookie teammate Kimi Antonelli dominate the 2026 F1 driver standings after three Grands Prix, with Antonelli leading at 72 points to Russell's 63 following his Chinese GP victory and strong Japanese GP qualifying pole, where Russell suffered a battery failure and Safety Car misfortune that cost a podium. Their W17 car's superior race pace and qualifying speed have opened a 45-point constructors' lead over Ferrari, sidelining contenders like Charles Leclerc (49 points) and Lewis Hamilton (41 points at Ferrari). Trader consensus prices Russell as slight favorite at 43.5% implied probability on his experience and early Australian GP win, while Antonelli's 30.8% reflects his rapid adaptation despite intra-team pressure from Toto Wolff's green light to race freely, barring contact.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's 40% implied probability as 2026 NBA Champion frontrunners stems from their league-best 62-16 record, clinched No. 1 Western Conference seed, and status as defending champions, fueled by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP frontrunner campaign and elite depth amid a dominant regular season. San Antonio Spurs at 16% reflect their strong 60-19 mark and No. 2 West position, powered by Victor Wembanyama's two-way impact in a breakout sophomore year. Boston Celtics' 12.2% share holds on Eastern Conference contention despite a 53-25 record, while Denver Nuggets (9.5%) benefit from Nikola Jokić's triple-double averages despite injury concerns; Detroit Pistons (5.6%) surge with the East's top 57-22 mark. Recent trade deadline moves and G League recalls like OKC's Nikola Topic bolster playoff rotations as the postseason nears.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: FC Bayern München (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Bayern München" if FC Bayern München win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Real Madrid CF".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Real Madrid and Bayern Munich head into their UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg on April 7 at the Bernabéu amid injury crises tilting trader sentiment toward a competitive tie. Madrid's Thibaut Courtois is ruled out for both legs with a thigh injury, forcing Andriy Lunin into goal, while Rodrygo remains sidelined long-term by an ACL tear and Éder Militão nears return from a muscle issue around April 1. Bayern faces a goalkeeper nightmare with Manuel Neuer (calf), Sven Ulreich (adductor), and Jonas Urbig (concussion) unavailable, plus Jamal Musiala out with a fibula injury. Madrid's home advantage and recent La Liga momentum counter Bayern's strong aggregate win over Atalanta, but vulnerabilities on both benches heighten upset potential.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Celtics host the Charlotte Hornets at TD Garden with a spotless injury report—the first all season—featuring Jayson Tatum, Jaylen Brown, and Derrick White all available amid their strong 53-25 Eastern Conference standing. The Hornets (43-36), aiming for a fifth straight win to bolster playoff positioning, contend with key absences: Tidjane Salaun out (calf), PJ Hall sidelined (ankle), and Coby White day-to-day (groin). Boston's home dominance, superior record, and full health contrast Charlotte's injury concerns and road test, driving trader consensus toward the Celtics in this late-season clash with seeding implications.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Preliminary results from Colombia's March 8, 2026, parliamentary elections, as tallied by the Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil, position the Historic Pact for Colombia (PH) first with 42 seats in the 188-member Chamber of Representatives, followed by Centro Democrático (CD) at 30 seats, and Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) at 26 seats—well ahead of the Partido Conservador Colombiano's 19. With 100% of votes verified in 31 of 32 departments by the National Electoral Council, traders price PLC's third-place finish at overwhelming odds, reflecting the insurmountable gap under proportional representation from multi-member constituencies and reserved seats. Only a major anomaly in the final department's certification or successful legal challenge could shift allocations, though none have emerged in the past month.

title: Kansas City Royals vs. Cleveland Guardians
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Kansas City Royals and Cleveland Guardians, scheduled for April 7 at 6:10PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Kansas City Royals" if the Kansas City Royals win the game.

This market will resolve to "Cleveland Guardians" if the Cleveland Guardians win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the AL Central matchup at Progressive Field, the Royals hold a narrow edge in trader consensus following their 4-2 series-opening win on April 6, powered by Michael Wacha's seven innings of one-run ball and solo homers from Carter Jensen and Jonathan India. Game two pits Kansas City's Noah Cameron (1-0, 1.80 ERA) against Cleveland's Gavin Williams (1-1, 2.25 ERA), both delivering early-season quality starts amid thin early lineups. Guardians miss shortstop Gabriel Arias (10-day IL) and outfielder George Valera (calf), while Royals' bullpen lacks Carlos Estevez (foot bruise); both clubs hover near .500 with balanced home/away splits. Weather-neutral conditions favor a low-scoring pitchers' duel ahead of Wednesday's rubber match.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chicago Bulls (29-49) visit the Washington Wizards (17-61) amid mutual injury crises and extended losing streaks, with Chicago dropping seven straight after a narrow 120-110 defeat to Phoenix on April 5 and Washington falling 121-115 at Brooklyn the same day. Bulls injury report lists Matas Buzelis, Josh Giddey, Anfernee Simons (wrist), Zach Collins, and others out, while Wizards rule out Alex Sarr (toe), Tre Johnson (foot), Bilal Coulibaly, and up to 10 total, forcing reliance on rookies like Will Riley. Both lottery teams prioritize draft positioning over late-season wins, though Chicago's superior record and road form give trader consensus a slight Bulls lean in this matchup at Capital One Arena.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US military strikes targeted Iranian defenses and facilities on Kharg Island, Iran's vital Persian Gulf oil export hub handling over 90% of its crude shipments, in the past 24 hours amid escalating US-Iran tensions under President Trump's demands for nuclear concessions and Strait of Hormuz reopening. The attacks spared oil infrastructure, with Tehran asserting continued control and uninterrupted exports despite reported power outages. Earlier March strikes and Iranian defensive buildups have heightened seizure risks, but no ground operations or loss of control have occurred. Traders watch Trump's impending deadline for diplomacy or further escalation, including potential amphibious assaults, as key catalysts in this active conflict.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 97.3% implied probability for no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting the Federal Reserve's steady 3.50%-3.75% target range affirmed in March amid resilient economic data. March nonfarm payrolls rose 178,000—exceeding forecasts—with unemployment dipping to 4.3%, bolstering views of a robust labor market and inflation above the 2% target without immediate policy shifts. This positions the "no change" outcome as near-consensus, backed by real capital at stake. Realistic challenges include hotter-than-expected April CPI or escalating geopolitical oil shocks prompting reassessment, though FOMC minutes on April 8 offer the next sentiment gauge ahead of the decision.

title: Oilers vs. Utah
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 7 at 9:30PM ET:
If the Oilers win, the market will resolve to "Oilers".
If the Utah win, the market will resolve to "Utah".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers face Utah Mammoth in a pivotal late-season clash at Delta Center, with both teams jockeying for playoff positioning amid high stakes in the Western Conference. Oilers (39-29-9, 87 points) sit tied atop the Pacific Division but enter on a sour note after a 5-1 loss to Vegas snapped their five-game win streak, compounded by major injuries: Leon Draisaitl sidelined through the regular season with a lower-body issue and Zach Hyman out for this road trip due to a nagging ailment, thinning their top-six forward group despite Colton Dach's LTIR return. Utah (40-30-6, 86 points), clinging to the first wild card spot, rides momentum from a 7-4 road win over Vancouver and boasts home-ice edge plus a stingy penalty kill (78.8%). Oilers lead the season series 2-0 and hold a perfect 5-0 historical mark, but trader consensus reflects Utah's advantages in health, rest, and playoff desperation for this potential first-round preview.

This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final High price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT High prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

title: Baltimore Orioles vs. Chicago White Sox
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Baltimore Orioles and Chicago White Sox, scheduled for April 7 at 7:40PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Baltimore Orioles" if the Baltimore Orioles win the game.

This market will resolve to "Chicago White Sox" if the Chicago White Sox win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Baltimore's 2-1 win over the White Sox on Monday—snapping a three-game skid behind spot starter Brandon Young's crisp outing—Tuesday's series finale at Guaranteed Rate Field pits Orioles lefty Trevor Rogers (2-0, 13 IP) against Chicago's Shane Smith (0-2). Both clubs stand at 4-6 early in the AL season, with the Orioles holding a 1-0 edge and recent head-to-head dominance. White Sox outfielder Austin Hays is day-to-day with a hamstring tweak from the loss, joining Everson Pereira (ankle, IL), while Baltimore placed reliever Dietrich Enns on the IL (infected foot). Cold Chicago weather and bullpen depth will influence this low-scoring affair between evenly matched rotations.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no regime collapse in Iran by April 30, with "No" at 96.3%, driven by the Islamic Republic's institutional resilience amid US-Israeli airstrikes that killed Supreme Leader Khamenei in early March and degraded military infrastructure. An interim leadership council, including Ayatollah Alireza Arafi and President Masoud Pezeshkian, has maintained command, backed by Revolutionary Guard loyalty and recent executions to preempt uprisings from economic crisis-fueled protests. US intelligence assessments as of mid-March confirm no imminent fall, despite Strait of Hormuz closure and ongoing escalation. Realistic shifts could stem from total infrastructure devastation, mass defections, or a successful opposition surge before resolution, though historical patterns favor regime survival under pressure.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's duo Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen top Polymarket trader consensus at 38.5% implied probability to win Eurovision 2026, propelled by their UMK-winning entry "Liekinheitin"—a high-energy blend of violin virtuosity and pop charisma that balances jury appeal with televote potential, as evidenced by leading OGAE fan poll scores and surging YouTube views since its late February release. France (10.7%) and Denmark (10.3%) trail closely, buoyed by jury-favorite entries like France's sophisticated ballad and Denmark's emotive "Før Vi Går Hjem," while Greece (6.5%) gains from Akylas's "Ferto" post-national final buzz. The April 2 semi-final running order reveal—placing Finland mid-pack for optimal impact—has solidified this hierarchy amid high-volume trading ($68M+), though upsets remain possible with Vienna semis on May 12-14 and the grand final May 16.

title: Lightning vs. Senators
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 7 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Lightning win, the market will resolve to "Lightning".
If the Senators win, the market will resolve to "Senators".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tampa Bay Lightning face Ottawa Senators tonight at Canadian Tire Centre, with Tampa (48-23-6, 102 points) leading the Atlantic Division and Ottawa (40-27-10, 90 points) battling for a wild card berth amid late-season playoff implications. Ottawa's blueline is severely depleted by injuries to Thomas Chabot (forearm surgery), Tyler Kleven (upper body), Carter Yakemchuk (concussion), Dennis Gilbert, and Nick Jensen, though Jake Sanderson returns, pressuring Linus Ullmark in net. Tampa counters absences of Victor Hedman (personal), Brandon Hagel (undisclosed), Anthony Cirelli, and Pontus Holmberg (upper body), but boasts offensive firepower from Nikita Kucherov, Brayden Point, and Jake Guentzel against Andrei Vasilevskiy. Lightning won their most recent head-to-head 4-2 on March 28, highlighting Tampa's edge in forward depth and recent form.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder head to Los Angeles as heavy favorites against a depleted Lakers squad on April 7, with trader consensus reflecting OKC's dominant 62-16 record atop the Western Conference standings and a 3-0 head-to-head edge this season, capped by their 139-96 rout of LA just five days ago. Lakers stars Luka Dončić (Grade 2 hamstring), Austin Reaves (oblique), and Marcus Smart (ankle) are confirmed out through at least the regular season finale, while LeBron James remains questionable with ongoing foot management; Thunder counter with Jalen Williams sidelined for hamstring injury management but boast full depth led by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber form. Lakers' home-court advantage offers slim upset potential amid late-season playoff positioning battles, but OKC's rest edge and momentum shape the implied probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Minnesota Timberwolves head to Indiana as heavy trader favorites despite missing star Anthony Edwards and Jaden McDaniels due to injuries, facing a severely depleted Pacers roster with nine players sidelined, including Tyrese Haliburton (Achilles tear), Pascal Siakam (ankle), Andrew Nembhard (back), T.J. McConnell (hamstring), and Aaron Nesmith (neck). Indiana's 18-60 record reflects a injury-ravaged season, forcing a G-League-caliber lineup of Quenton Jackson, Ethan Thompson, Kobe Brown, Jarace Walker, and Jay Huff, while Minnesota counters with Mike Conley, Donte DiVincenzo, Ayo Dosunmu, Julius Randle, and Rudy Gobert. A Timberwolves win could clinch their No. 6 Western Conference seed for the playoffs amid recent tailspin concerns, though Pacers' home underdog ATS success adds upset potential.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sporting CP face Arsenal in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg on April 7 at Estádio José Alvalade, with the hosts severely hampered by key absences: winger Nuno Santos sidelined by a thigh injury for around three weeks and captain Morten Hjulmand suspended, eroding defensive width and midfield control against Arsenal's high-pressing style. Both could return for the April 15 second leg at Emirates Stadium. Arsenal navigate their own injury concerns post-Carabao Cup final loss to Manchester City, with Mikel Merino out (foot), Eberechi Eze doubtful (month sidelined), and Martin Ødegaard, Jurrien Timber, Leandro Trossard as question marks, though no fresh issues reported recently. Arsenal boast nine UCL wins from ten this season, while Sporting reach their first quarter-final amid strong Primeira Liga form but recent fixture rescheduling for extra preparation. Home advantage and aggregate scoring favor a cautious Sporting approach, but trader consensus likely reflects Arsenal's edge in squad depth and momentum.
